Lisa L. Oberg, Esq., and Felicia Feng, Esq., of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation in all three cases
Andrew Chew, Esq., and Anne T. Acuna, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Gerald Burr, Sr. (#275287), Frank Delahaye (#275327) and Sherman Fong (#275310)

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.'s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1, 2, 4 and 5 (general negligence, strict liability design and manufacturing defect, civil battery and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). Defendant has satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. Defendant has satisfied its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant committed civil battery and/or acted with malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence and plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on their claim for civil battery or request for punitive damages. Plaintiffs represents that they dismissed their claim for false representation. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
